meta.title: Jonathan Brownstein — Macroeconomic Modeling Specialist
meta.description: Contract econometrician and forecasting specialist with fifteen years in regulated financial services and global industrial organizations, including seven years building production time series models, forecast validation

typingPhrases: Macroeconomic Modeling and Time Series Forecasting
hero.description: Contract econometrician and forecasting specialist with fifteen years in regulated financial services and global industrial organizations, including seven years building production time series models, forecast validation workflows, and executive reporting for actuarial and finance leadership. MS in Economics and a graduate certificate in Applied Econometrics with graduate training in time series forecasting, multivariate econometric methods, and model diagnostics. Designed and operated an automated ARIMA forecasting platform on a scheduled production pipeline, with governed documentation and accuracy review before results reach decision makers. Hands on in Python, R, SAS, and SQL. Available immediately for remote part time contract work.

highlights.label: Macroeconomic modeling, time series forecasting, ARIMA, applied econometrics, model validation, model diagnostics, forecast accuracy review, cointegration and multivariate time series methods, statistical outlier detection, period over period movement analysis, automated forecasting workflows, econometric scripting in Python and R, financial sector analytics, executive ready written reporting, remote contract delivery

items.title: Time Series Forecasting and Econometric Modeling Specialist
items.organization: Global life and health reinsurance, remote contract
bullets: Designed and deployed an automated ARIMA based forecasting platform for Net Amount at Risk on a scheduled production pipeline, with model validation, anomaly review, and trend reporting used by actuarial and finance leadership before period close.

Built repeatable period over period valuation movement analysis across time periods and source systems, with documented methodology and audit friendly outputs other teams extended for variance and trend work.

Developed statistical outlier and anomaly detection frameworks using probability density function methods across platforms processing more than a trillion records, supporting forecast reliability and early warning on attribute drift.

Authored model lifecycle documentation and methodology summaries for business and governance reviewers, translating statistical output into plain language narratives for mixed technical and non technical audiences.

items.title: FP&A and Forecasting Analytics Consultant
bullets: Owned annual planning, monthly forecasting, and executive reporting cycles for finance, sales, operations, and regional leadership inside a global multidivisional organization.
Designed automated KPI dashboards and reporting frameworks that replaced manual reconciliation work and shortened month end forecast commentary cycles.
Led cross functional reporting modernization that improved data consistency and validation checkpoints before numbers reached executive sponsors.

groups.title: Econometrics and forecasting
items: ARIMA, time series forecasting, applied econometrics, model diagnostics, forecast validation, cointegration and multivariate time series methods, statistical modeling

groups.title: Languages and tools
items: Python (pandas, NumPy, statsmodels, scikit learn), R, SAS, SQL, advanced Excel and VBA

groups.title: Delivery
items: automated forecasting pipelines, governed model documentation, Power BI, Tableau, Databricks, remote part time contract availability
